sentences.text: Morning, Megan.
Good morning.
Morning, Cory.
Good morning, Alec.
Morning.
I don't know how we feel about the recording, I guess we could ask the group this morning.
I was about to shut it off.
Do you want me to leave it and see if they say anything?
I assume we can shut it off, is that fair, Megan?
I… I recorded with Chip last time, so I….
Well, let's leave it, and we'll ask their permission once they join.
Brian might be more sensitive, but Chip was cool with it.
We have Chip and Trent in the meeting room right now.
Trent's on our team.
… If we do some introductions, how are we…
Well, we'll let everyone introduce themselves.
Yeah.
Hey, Trent, morning.
Morning.
Alright, Chip and Brian in the meeting… in the waiting room right now.
Everybody okay with what was sent over, and okay to let them in?
Cool.
Here they come.
Hello, everyone!
Hey, guys.
Hey, Chip.
Hey, Brian.
Christina, hello?
How's it going?
It's hump day, so a lot going on.
The weekend feels close already, long weekend.
Excited for that.
I myself just came back from a few days out of office, so I'm already looking forward to the next, as you, as you can see, already looking forward to the next one.
What Phil meant to say is things are great here.
How are, … how are you all doing?
Can't complain.
Not complain.
I was actually just out of office myself.
I'm looking forward to the long weekend as well, Phil.
So, we're in, we're in similar boats there.
We're in cahoots, Chip.
I appreciate that.
Any place Fun Chip?
I was actually in, France and then Spain, so… For once, yes, it was actually fun.
Usually it's like, no, I'm in Washington, D.
C, or Boston, which is not nearly as fun.
France and Spain, no big deal, yeah, … I bet it was fun.
That's awesome.
And if we see a number that's joined, maybe that's Joan?
Up, Brian.
No, that's Brian.
Sorry, I'm traveling, so I'm sitting in a lobby.
No, no problem.
Not video, but on the phone.
And we've designed this, I think, largely as a product feedback session.
Brian, any issues with the recording today?
Happy to turn it off, per usual, if you'd prefer.
If it's a training video, that's… that's fine, typically.
Okay, … well, I, I think, … Yeah, it… I think what we'd prob… it sounds… why don't we turn it off, Phil?
And, it sounds like, let's just err on the side of.
